profile.name: Su Yechun (xieyezi)
profile.title: Senior Frontend Engineer | 7 years of experience | Male | 30 years old | Chongqing

parsec.company: Parsec Technology Co., Ltd. (Senior Frontend Engineer | Remote)
responsibilitiesItems: Developed Pietra Creator Admin and Pietra Landing Page (official website)
Developed AI tools such as Pietra AI Copilot and Sourcing AI Agent
Built internal engineering plugins and tools (e.g. Pietra Devtool) to improve team developer experience (DX)
engineeringItems: Built the @pietra/auto-import plugin based on AST and Vite, enabling automatic imports of components (including internal and third-party components), icons, and utility libraries (e.g. Lodash), eliminating the need for manual imports and significantly improving developer productivity

Due to complex business logic and heavy data collection requirements, manual tracking implementation introduced high code intrusion. To solve this, developed the @pietra-auto-tracker plugin using AST, enabling zero-intrusion automatic tracking during build time, significantly improving data collection efficiency and reducing business code coupling

Developed Pietra Devtool, which runs as an independent DevTool frontend process in development mode. It communicates with the Vite dev server via RPC and centralizes project metadata, build graphs, and resources into a single dashboard, solving the issue of fragmented debugging information such as module dependencies, static assets (images, CSS variables, custom components, hooks, etc.), and build processes
architectureItems: Refactored the official website to Next.js, implementing SSR (Server-Side Rendering) and ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration), reducing first-page load time and improving SEO traffic
Bundle optimization using tree-shaking and dynamic imports, reducing production bundle size by 67%
Improved Google Lighthouse scores by optimizing LCP, FCP, and CLS metrics; Search Console score increased from 40 to 90+

responsibilitiesItems: Development of SDK APIs for various automotive product lines, including SDK API
Upgraded legacy frontend architecture to a new architecture and resolved historical technical debt
Developed Incall CLI to improve team development workflow and productivity
Conducted frontend technical interviews and code reviews
architectureItems: Upgraded project architecture to a Monorepo structure, consolidating distributed codebases into a unified and standardized management system. Introduced component-based design to decouple business logic and extract reusable base and business component libraries, while implementing on-demand loading based on routes and modules, significantly reducing initial bundle size and improving application performance and user experience

Led the refactoring of the vehicle control SDK into a micro-frontend architecture, splitting tightly coupled features into independent micro-applications supporting independent development, deployment, and on-demand loading. Designed core architecture including micro-app lifecycle, host communication mechanisms, and dynamic loading strategies. Built remote update capabilities enabling OTA-like delivery, canary releases, and seamless upgrades without app store redeployment. This solution has been deployed across multiple host apps (Changan Auto, Deepal, Kaicene, etc.), effectively reducing bundle size and improving iteration efficiency

Led technology stack upgrades from Webpack 4 to Webpack 5/Vite, and migration from Vue2 to Vue2.7/Vue3. Established unified development standards and build systems. Developed engineering tools such as VSCode plugins and ESLint/Prettier configurations, achieving automated code standards and standardized development workflows, significantly improving DX including build speed, development efficiency, and code consistency while reducing onboarding time for new engineers

Performed fine-grained performance optimization from build system to resource loading layer. Refactored complex Webpack configurations into modular and function-based structures for better maintainability and scalability. Developed custom Webpack loaders for business-specific compilation optimization. Integrated CDN-based static asset delivery and optimized entry dependency structure, reducing initial bundle size and improving overall loading performance
teamBuildingItems: Established a centralized technical documentation system to standardize development practices
Organized internal technical sharing sessions to improve overall team capability
Represented the company at technical conferences and shared engineering experience

javascript.title: Proficient in frontend development using JavaScript (ES6) / TypeScript, with strong experience in the following frameworks
items: React state management: Zustand / Jotai / Redux
React component libraries: Radix UI / Ant Design

css.title: Proficient in CSS3 and related tools
items: Solutions: CSS Modules / Styled Components / Emotion

build.title: Strong understanding of frontend engineering and build tools
items: Experienced in using Webpack / Vite for web application bundling and performance optimization
Proficient in using Rollup for component library packaging and publishing
Familiar with code quality tools such as Prettier and ESLint

git.title: Proficient in Git for version control and code management, familiar with Git flow

advantages.title: Personal Strengths
items: Featured author on Juejin (technical blogging platform)
Strong proficiency in JavaScript, TypeScript, ES6
Contributor to multiple open-source projects (Vant, Semi-Design, Vue, etc.)
Extensive experience in frontend frameworks (React, Next.js, Vue), compiler systems, and performance optimization
Former frontend architect leading team architecture design and performance optimization, built in-house scaffolding tools
Invited speaker at the 2022 Chongqing Frontend Technology Conference
Holder of two invention patents (CN115269034A and CN115686570A)

metadata.title: Su Yechun (xieyezi) - Frontend Engineer | Resume
metadata.description: Resume of Su Yechun (xieyezi), a frontend engineer specializing in modern frontend technologies including React, Next.js, TypeScript, and more.
